masthead.headline: Investigaciones
masthead.subtitle: Data analysis and investigative reporting on 23 years of Mexican federal procurement

el-ejercito-fantasma.headline: The Ghost Army: 6,034 Vendors That Look Like Nothing
el-ejercito-fantasma.sub: RUBLI found 6,034 ghost-pattern vendors · only 42 officially confirmed
el-ejercito-fantasma.brief: RUBLI identified 6,034 vendors matching ghost company patterns across 23 years of Mexican federal procurement. They appear, win contracts, then vanish from the tax registry.

el-gran-precio.headline: The Bigger the Contract, the Higher the Risk
el-gran-precio.sub: 23,469 large contracts · MX$6.24T · avg risk score 0.70
el-gran-precio.brief: RUBLI's risk model reveals a near-monotonic ladder across 3 million contracts: as contract size grows, corruption risk grows in lockstep. The biggest deals carry the most risk.

el-monopolio-invisible.headline: The 44 Monopolists: Vendors Who Ate Their Sector
el-monopolio-invisible.sub: Grupo Fármacos · MX$133B · 6,303 contracts · one institution
el-monopolio-invisible.brief: Four pharmaceutical distributors collected 326 billion pesos from IMSS over 23 years with no meaningful competition. Their combined risk score averages 0.96.

la-ilusion-competitiva.headline: The Competition That Never Was
la-ilusion-competitiva.brief: For 14 straight years, over 45% of Mexico's "competitive" procurement had exactly one bidder. The OECD flags anything above 15% as a structural red flag.

captura-institucional.headline: Inside Institutional Capture: 15,923 Vendors, 787B in Three Agencies
captura-institucional.sub: IMSS · CFE · PEMEX · P6 pattern at Mexico's largest institutions
captura-institucional.brief: 15,923 vendors show behavioral capture signatures at IMSS, CFE, PEMEX, SCT, and CONAGUA. That's nearly a trillion pesos of systematically captured contracting.

marea-de-adjudicaciones.headline: The Direct Award Tide: From 60% to 82% in 13 Years
marea-de-adjudicaciones.sub: 82.2% non-competitive in 2023 · every administration worse than the last
marea-de-adjudicaciones.brief: 82.2% of 2023 federal contracts were direct awards — no competition, no public tender. Every administration since 2010 has been worse than the one before it.

el-sexenio-del-riesgo.headline: The Riskiest Administration in 23 Years
el-sexenio-del-riesgo.sub: AMLO era HR 17.6% · Fox 7.9% · Calderón 9.7% · Peña Nieto 12.4%
el-sexenio-del-riesgo.brief: AMLO's 17.6% high-risk rate is the first in 23 years to breach the OECD ceiling. Four administrations, two parties — one relentlessly ascending line.

la-industria-del-intermediario.headline: The Intermediary Industry: 2,974 Vendors, 518B MXN
la-industria-del-intermediario.sub: Infrastructure 174B · Health 129B · Energy 121B · RUBLI P3 pattern
la-industria-del-intermediario.brief: 2,974 vendors function as pure procurement intermediaries — no physical product, no technical service, just margin extraction between government agencies and real suppliers.

el-umbral-de-los-300k.headline: The 300,000 Peso Threshold Trap
el-umbral-de-los-300k.sub: 75,474 contracts at exactly MX$300K · statistical impossibility without coordination
el-umbral-de-los-300k.brief: Statistical spikes at 210K, 250K, and 300K MXN — mathematically impossible in a random pricing universe. The evidence points to systematic threshold manipulation to avoid competitive bidding requirements.

volatilidad-el-precio-del-riesgo.headline: Price Volatility: The Algorithm's Smoking Gun
volatilidad-el-precio-del-riesgo.sub: Strongest predictor in v0.6.5 model · coefficient +0.5343 across 3M contracts
